description: All Infiltrator tools: Standard Kit (always carried, no slot), the 9 loadout gadgets (pick 3 at setup), and the found-item pool (4-6 spawn per round on item anchors). Reference data for Phases 10/12 — not runtime-loaded yet.

slots.idCardSlotRule: Separate single-capacity slot for one looted badge; looting a second forces drop-and-swap. Never costs a gadget slot.
slots.pickupRule: Picking up a found item means dropping a current gadget in its place; dropped gadgets persist in the world.

standardKit.note: Feeds the same visibility sampling as every other light source — raises the carrier's own light level. Off by default.

doorForce.noisePerSwing: one independent notice-roll per guard in plausible earshot, per swing

takedown.requires: inside melee range AND outside target's vision cone
takedown.result: target Down, silent, no report enqueued, badge lootable
takedown.failureMode: clear sighting inside melee range = immediate capture (guard rule, not tool rule)
takedown.vsTechnician: works only before flee state engages

standardKit.signal: directional static burst on guard radio key-up — never report text
standardKit.timing: fires on key-up, not report completion; both players learn of a transmission on the same frame

loadoutGadgets.note: Short range, silent. The only violence available while carrying the drive.

loadoutGadgets.lumenTell: delayed looped-feed alert, no nodeId — quiet and eventually vague
immediate camera-lost alert with nodeId — loud and specific

loadoutGadgets.note: Drives a real event onto the Phase 2 sound bus; doubles as a deliberate false alarm.
Spoofs a sensor node without the Infiltrator being present. Pure misdirection.
The considered alternative to the pry bar's noise. Exists to buy the pry-bar risk away — Phase 17 checks it stays worth a slot.
Beats heat sensors specifically — not visual cameras, guards' eyes, or microphones.
Never tied to a real person, so it can't be flagged the way a looted badge can. Face/ID-verifying cameras still catch it.
The only tool aimed at LUMEN directly. Suppression happens at the source, not the UI (Phase 9).

foundItems.effect: fast one-way traversal to a slow/high route
blocked line of sight in a small area for a few seconds
one-time sprint boost without the noise penalty
labels every room role + badge door in one wing
foundItems.placementRule: revealed wing biased away from spawn wing (Phase 12)

foundItems.effect: labels every room role, door type, and sabotage fixture facility-wide
foundItems.placementRule: never spawns inside a room it would reveal as a vault; strongest item in the pool

foundItems.effect: pins the nearest two Warden-placed sensors to the blueprint
foundItems.note: the only found item that reveals deployment rather than architecture

foundItems.effect: slide under any closed door (badge-gated included) to view the room behind it for a few seconds
foundItems.note: sees, never opens — the escalation of the baseline Peek verb, which only works at authored apertures

constraints.originPing: delayed zone-level ping on Facility Deployment — guaranteed, unblockable

foundItems.placementRule: rarest entry; never placed in the same round as a Maintenance schematic (Phase 12)

foundItems.effect: downed guards can be dragged out of sightlines
longer-duration Ghost pass at the cost of a full slot
+1 tranquilizer shot, stacks with base loadout

spawnRules.spreadConstraint: never all in one wing; never all on the shortest entrance-to-vault path
